body_txt: Characters that die , get eaten , go on a long trip , spontaneously vanish , or otherwise exit the show — and all before we get to properly know them. These characters might be brought Back for the Dead or have someone drop a bridge on them . Writers might reassign their arc to an existing character or to a totally new character . It may not be a writer's intention to get rid of a character, but the actor who portrays them may want out sooner than expected and plans must be redrawn. These situations may lead to Commuting on a Bus . Can become a Posthumous Character , averting this, if they remain dead but they're still developed via flashbacks or similar means. On elimination-based game shows this usually applies to a contestant who is the first one to go unless they reappear in the series later on. Compare Sacrificial Lamb , Shoo Out the New Guy . If the character was a hardly-seen Recurring Extra who finally gets A Day in the Limelight only to be killed off, that's A Death in the Limelight . Might result in the cast itself forgetting their fallen friend. If the character is liked enough, fans may lament their very short appearances and believe that They Wasted a Perfectly Good Character . NOTE: This is a Death Trope or at least a trope about a character exit. Unmarked spoilers ahoy.
